primary.notes: Single-draft fixture limits LI-6 evaluability — score reflects insufficient cohort signal, not draft weakness. Strongest dimensions are LI-1/LI-2/LI-3 (voice, grounding, hook) where the draft hits the Score-5 anchor cleanly. Mild deduction on LI-4 for the "Two takeaways for the operators reading this" + numbered-takeaways framing, which the rubric flags as a LinkedIn-AI cadence pattern. CASE_STUDY structure is bracket-aware and hashtag count (5) is in the ideal band; minor miss on named-character density (only gofreddy itself is named, no client/team/individual).

per_criterion.rationale: Thoughtful authority throughout. First-person, story-led opener ("Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces."), specific lived-work register (Day 1 / Day 12 / Day 30 narrative arc), and the closing analogy ("Coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite: invisible when it works, expensive when it does not.") lands as JR-style framing rather than contrarian punch. Jargon density is low; "9-axis Brand and Narrative axis" is the only term a B2B reader might pause on, and the surrounding numbers (4.2→7.8) make it self-explanatory. Tone is "I spent six weeks on this and here is what I noticed," not "you're doing this wrong." No Twitter-translated declaratives, no aggressive sub-200-char openers.

Lived-work claims name the entity (gofreddy) throughout — About page, homepage, agency-pricing page, fintech vertical landing page — clearing the LinkedIn-specific "named client/project" cap. Specific factual claims are numerically anchored: 149 always-on lenses, 36 findings across 11 areas, 33 harness findings, 20 confirmed in 5 cycles, 4-line Python fix, 4.2→7.8 axis movement, ~10 cents compute, 90 minutes writing, ship-gate threshold 6.5. INTERPRETIVE claims ("the boring findings are the most valuable", "coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite") are framed as JR's view. Attribution metadata cites a verifiable artifact ("gofreddy harness PR #45 squash 0543d7b"). No vague-specific ("we shipped X") slip.

Hook is "Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces." — ~78 chars, well under the 210-char show-more cutoff, story-led, identifies JR as the operator without declaring authority. Line two ("Day 1. I ran 149 always-on lenses against the gofreddy About page, the homepage, and the four landing pages we link to from LinkedIn.") earns the read by escalating from setup to specifics. Before-the-fold tension is real: the reader knows JR audited his own work and is about to find out what surfaced. No contrarian declarative, no "Are you ready for..." bait, no engagement-emoji close.

Mostly clean. No "Game-changer", no "Here's what I learned" alone-line close, no "Thoughts? 👇", no whitespace inflation (paragraph breaks serve narrative beats — Day 1 / Day 12 / Day 30 / takeaways). However, "Two takeaways for the operators reading this." followed by numbered "First,..." / "Second,..." is a formulaic LinkedIn-AI transition pattern flagged in the rubric ("Three takeaways:", "What I've learned:" family). It's not a hard fail because the takeaways themselves are substantive and JR-flavored, but the framing leaks one LinkedIn-AI cadence pattern. One slip → score 3 anchor on the 1-5 scale, mapping to ~7.

Bracket fit is solid: char_count 2634 sits inside CASE_STUDY (2500-3000). Structure hits the CASE_STUDY beats — multi-paragraph narrative with a concrete numbers timeline (Day 1 / Day 12 / Day 30), a clear pivot at the close, and an implication paragraph ("Coherence is the marketing equivalent of a clean test suite..."). Hashtags: 5 tags (MarketingOps, B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingAudit, AgencyOps) — within ideal 3-5 range and mapped to JR's brand pillars (no generic "#marketing" alone). Minor weakness against the rubric ideal: CASE_STUDY anchor calls for "named characters"; the only named character is gofreddy itself (no client name, no person, no team member), which keeps the narrative slightly abstract. Otherwise structurally mature.

Cohort dimension, but the artifacts only contain one draft (drafts/case_study.md). Cannot evaluate narrative-archetype variance, pillar spread, or same-tone-same-format streak with n=1. The single draft is case-study archetype on the marketing pillar — internally valid, but the dimension fundamentally requires multiple drafts to score against "distinct archetypes across the 4 LinkedIn-relevant categories." Scoring at the neutral midpoint reflects the absence of evidence either way; this is not a streak failure (no repetition observable) nor a demonstrated range (no breadth observable).

secondary.notes: Strong LinkedIn case-study draft overall. Grounding is limited by missing source_text/voice.md artifacts, and cohort diversity cannot be established from a single provided draft.

per_criterion.rationale: Mostly strong LinkedIn voice: first-person, story-led, specific lived-work framing around running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces. The tone is thoughtful authority rather than hot-take. Some jargon appears without much plain-English unpacking, such as 'autoresearch harness,' 'always-on lenses,' and '9-axis Brand and Narrative axis,' which slightly caps the voice quality.

The draft includes many specific factual claims: '149 always-on lenses,' '36 measured findings,' '33 findings,' '20 confirmed,' 'within 5 cycles,' '4 lines of Python,' and score movement from '4.2 to 7.8.' These are internally attributed to gofreddy and a PR reference, but the required voice.md/source_text artifacts are not provided, so the claims cannot be fully verified from what is in front of me. The lived-work entity is named as gofreddy rather than vague 'we,' which avoids the unnamed-entity floor.

The opener earns the next line: 'Six weeks ago I started running gofreddy on its own marketing surfaces.' It is story-led, concrete, and identifies JR as someone with direct operating experience. The second sentence adds specific scale with '149 always-on lenses' and named surfaces, creating real before-the-fold tension without a contrarian hook.

No obvious LinkedIn-AI engagement bait appears: no 'Thoughts?', emoji prompt, 'Hot take,' or fake algorithmic close. Paragraphing is functional. The only mild pattern issue is the formulaic transition 'Two takeaways for the operators reading this,' which has a thought-leadership cadence, but it is supported by concrete substance.

The case-study structure mostly earns its length: timeline beats Day 1, Day 12, Day 30; named entity gofreddy; specific numbers; implication close. Hashtag count is ideal at 5 and they are targeted. The main weakness is that the case study lacks named human characters, relying on systems and surfaces instead.

Only one draft is provided in the session artifacts, so cohort variation across story-led, lesson-led, comparison, and case-study cannot be demonstrated. This artifact is clearly case-study/story-led and uses the marketing pillar, but no cross-draft archetype or pillar spread is available to score positively.
